titles.title: Guard and Reserve GI Bill Parity Act of 2025
Guard and Reserve GI Bill Parity Act of 2025
A bill to amend title 38, United States Code, to expand eligibility for Post-9/11 Educational Assistance to members of the National Guard who perform certain full-time duty, and for other purposes.

action.text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs.
Committee on Veterans' Affairs. Hearings held. Hearings printed: S.Hrg. 119-86.
Committee on Veterans' Affairs. Ordered to be reported with an amendment in the nature of a substitute favorably.

committee.name: Senate Veterans' Affairs
committee.activity: Markup By, Hearings By (Full Committee), Hearings By (Full Committee), Referred To

bill.summary:
Guard and Reserve GI Bill Parity Act of 2025
This bill expands eligibility for Post-9/11 educational assistance for members of the reserve components of the Armed Forces and members of the National Guard. Specifically, the bill expands the types of activities that count towards Post-9/11 GI Bill eligibility to include active duty, inactive-duty training, annual training duty, and full-time National Guard duty or active duty. (Generally, under current law, only federal active duty counts towards educational assistance eligibility.)
